name: Vall d'Alcalà Pla de Petracos Trail
name_original: La Vall d'Ebo — Circular por la Sierra de Alfaro
description: This difficult 27.1-mile mountain biking trail in Pla de Petracos offers dramatic scenery, historic sites, and 3581 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around La Vall D'Ebo offers diverse and challenging terrain in the Marina Alta region of Alicante, Spain. The area is characterized by imposing mountains, deep valleys, and limestone cliffs, providing a dramatic backdrop for rides. Riders can navigate through serene pine forests and along meandering rivers, encountering significant elevation changes. This landscape provides a varied and engaging experience for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around La Vall D'Ebo The most popular mountain bike trail is La Vall de Laguar — Circular desde Pego por el Barranco del Infierno y Pla de Petracos, a 31.2 miles (50.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 12 minutes to complete. This challenging route features significant elevation gain and passes through historical sites. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vall d'Alcalà Pla de Petracos MTB Trail, a difficult 23.7 miles (38.2 km) path. This route offers varied terrain and considerable elevation, typical of the region's mountainous landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Vall d'Alcalà Pla de Petracos Trail, a 27.1 miles (43.7 km) trail leading through deep valleys and mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 48 minutes. Mountain biking around La Vall D'Ebo is defined by challenging climbs, limestone cliffs, and pine forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, including 6 easy, 12 moderate, and 30 difficult routes. The routes in La Vall D'Ebo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 650 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Vall D'Ebo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in La Vall D'Ebo?
A: La Vall D'Ebo offers diverse and challenging terrain, characterized by imposing mountains, deep valleys, and limestone cliffs. You'll navigate through serene pine forests and along meandering rivers, encountering significant elevation changes. The area's incredible orography, including features like the Barranc de l'Infern, provides a varied and engaging experience for mountain bikers.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in La Vall D'Ebo, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are 48 mountain bike trails in La Vall D'Ebo. These routes cater to various ability levels, including 6 easy, 12 moderate, and 30 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable challenge for most riders.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in La Vall D'Ebo?
A: Yes, La Vall D'Ebo features several circular mountain bike routes. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926019925/la-vall-de-laguar-circular-desde-pego-por-el-barranco-del-infierno-y-pla-de-petracos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vall de Laguar — Circular desde Pego por el Barranco del Infierno y Pla de Petracos&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 50 km and includes significant elevation gain, passing through historical sites.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while mountain biking in La Vall D'Ebo?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to see impressive natural gorges like the Barranc de l'Infern, also known as the 'cathedral of hiking.' Other notable features include the system of karst caves, such as the magical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/276237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Skulls&lt;/a&gt;, and stunning viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Foradà&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2916765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Cocoll&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the mountains and even the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in La Vall D'Ebo?
A: The mountain bike routes in La Vall D'Ebo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. Riders often praise the challenging climbs, diverse terrain, and dramatic scenery, making it a popular destination for those seeking an engaging outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike climbs in the area?
A: Absolutely. The Vall d'Ebo climb itself is legendary, known for its steep gradients averaging 6-8% and significant elevation gain. It's a true test of strength and endurance, attracting seasoned cyclists. Many of the difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e925804960/vall-d-alcala-pla-de-petracos-mtb-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vall d'Alcalà Pla de Petracos MTB Trail&lt;/a&gt;, feature considerable ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in La Vall D'Ebo?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's Mediterranean climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot, and winters mild, but it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through deep valleys or offer unique geological sights?
A: Yes, the region is known for its deep valleys and unique geological formations. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926041074/vall-d-alcala-pla-de-petracos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vall d'Alcalà Pla de Petracos Trail&lt;/a&gt; lead through deep valleys and mountainous terrain. The Barranc de l'Infern is a prime example of the area's impressive natural gorges, rich in biodiversity.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails with historical significance in La Vall D'Ebo?
A: Yes, some mountain bike routes in La Vall D'Ebo pass through areas with historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926019925/la-vall-de-laguar-circular-desde-pego-por-el-barranco-del-infierno-y-pla-de-petracos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vall de Laguar — Circular desde Pego por el Barranco del Infierno y Pla de Petracos&lt;/a&gt; route is known to feature historical points of interest along its challenging path.

Q: Are there any specific mountain passes that are popular among cyclists in La Vall D'Ebo?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/709871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vall d'Ebo Pass&lt;/a&gt; is a very popular and well-known climb among cyclists, including professionals. Other passes in the area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/380855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Creueta Pass (780 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/864629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Sorell&lt;/a&gt;, which offer challenging ascents and scenic views.

Q: What kind of views can I expect from the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often provide breathtaking panoramic views. From higher elevations, you can see the surrounding mountains, lush valleys, and even catch glimpses of the azure Mediterranean Sea in the distance. Viewpoints like Mirador del Montcabrer and Penya Forada are particularly renowned for their spectacular vistas.
tips: Breathtaking views from this vantage point at the top of the Sierra Alfaro. You can see the Malafí ravine, the Pla de Petracos and with good visibility to the Vall d'Ebo and beyond.

Paved road that runs along the Barranco de Malafi with a very picturesque landscape and environment. It allows to reach the beginning of the route of the cave paintings of Pla de Petracos, a renowned archaeological site and very interesting to see.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and terrain like for mountain biking on this trail?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult mountain bike trail, spanning approximately 43.7 km with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. The terrain in the Vall d'Alcalà and Pla de Petracos areas is diverse, featuring dry and dusty sections, technical rocky areas, and some paths with dirt and roots. You can expect punchy climbs with steep gradients, tight corners, and descents that require caution due to potential loose stones. While challenging, the descents can be very enjoyable.

faqs.question: What natural features and viewpoints can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers truly beautiful scenery, characterized by dramatic rock formations, olive and cherry trees, and Mediterranean landscapes. You'll encounter views of mountains and potentially coastal vistas. Notable points include the Sierra Alfaro Viewpoint and the scenic Vall d'Ebo Road. The region is also home to impressive natural landmarks like Penya La Foradà.

faqs.question: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area is rich in history. In the Pla de Petracos area, you can find prehistoric cave paintings, particularly along the descent towards Castell de Castells. The Vall d'Alcalà region also features ruins of Moorish settlements and ancient ice houses, offering unique historical and cultural insights.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vall d'Alcalà Pla de Petracos Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific trailhead parking for this exact route isn't detailed, the mountainous region of Alicante, including Vall d'Alcalà and Pla de Petracos, generally offers parking in nearby villages or designated areas. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most suitable parking options.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Public transport options directly to remote trailheads in the Vall d'Alcalà and Pla de Petracos areas can be limited. It's generally recommended to travel by car to access the starting points for mountain biking in this region. Some larger towns in the Costa Blanca region may have bus services, but connecting to specific trailheads might require further arrangements.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for mountain biking in Pla de Petracos?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails in the Vall d'Alcalà and Pla de Petracos areas. However, always respect private property and any local regulations or signage you may encounter, especially if passing through protected natural areas or near historical sites like the cave paintings.

faqs.question: Is the Vall d'Alcalà Pla de Petracos Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is in a natural, rural setting, specific regulations regarding dogs on mountain bike trails in this exact area are not explicitly defined. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always carry water for your dog and clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this trail is typically in early spring or late winter when the cherry trees are in bloom, offering particularly picturesque views. The Mediterranean climate means pleasant temperatures outside of the peak summer heat. Be aware that the terrain can be dry and dusty, and conditions may vary with rainfall.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The Vall d'Alcalà area is an ideal destination for rural and nature tourism, meaning you can find amenities in nearby villages. While specific cafes or pubs directly on the trail are unlikely, you can find options for food and accommodation (hotels, rural houses) in the surrounding towns and villages within the Vall d'Alcalà and Pla de Petracos regions.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other mountain biking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route covers significant sections of the "Vall d'Alcalà Pla de Petracos" trail (approximately 49% of its length). It also intersects with parts of other local routes such as "Benimassot - Barranc de Malafí - Castell de Castells - Benimassot", "La Sierra de la Aixortà", and various 'Camí' trails like "Camí de les Penyetes" and "Camí del Maser".

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficult rating and varied terrain, it's essential to bring appropriate gear. This includes a well-maintained mountain bike, helmet, gloves, plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), and a first-aid kit. Sun protection is also crucial due to the exposed Mediterranean landscape.
